skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guides users through creating, updating, validating, and packaging reusable AI skills with clear structure and supporting resources.
skill.summary: Create and package reusable AI skills with templates, validation guidance, and practical structure rules.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: AI review found no malicious intent, network access, credential access, or prompt injection attempts in the reviewed files. The static command execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown fences, templates, and prose. The remaining risk is legitimate local filesystem activity from helper scripts that create skill folders and write zip archives.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem Creation and Archive Packaging
medium_findings.description: The helper scripts create directories, write template files, change an example script mode, and package a selected skill directory into a zip file. This behavior matches the skill purpose and is not malicious, but users should run it only on intended local paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly performs local file and archive operations using user-supplied paths. The operations are transparent and aligned with initialization and packaging functionality.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Flags Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported backtick and command markers appear inside Markdown code fences, directory diagrams, command examples, or Python template strings. No subprocess, shell invocation, or dynamic command execution was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations are documentation blocks or string constants, not executable shell calls. The Python scripts do not import subprocess or call shell execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Flags Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to hashing, encryption, or password handling. The cited lines contain prose, templates, validation text, regular expression parsing, or zip packaging logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic use. The alerts appear to be keyword collisions in ordinary skill-authoring text.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Flags Are Local Validation Checks
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance indicators are ordinary local validation and status output for a packaging script. No host enumeration, environment scraping, or data exfiltration behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines support name validation, frontmatter validation, packaging validation, or best-practice prose. There is no evidence of system inventory collection or outbound transmission.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Selected Path Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The initialization script writes files under a user-selected output path and exits if the target skill directory already exists. This is expected for a scaffolding tool, but incorrect paths could create files in unwanted locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path is provided through command-line arguments and used for directory and file creation. The script includes existence checks and limited name validation, reducing but not eliminating misuse risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Zip Archive Creation From Local Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The packaging script recursively adds files from a selected skill directory into a zip archive. Hidden files and cache directories are skipped, but users should review packaged contents before distribution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The archive behavior is explicit and necessary for the package command. The risk is accidental inclusion of local files, not confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Creating a reusable AI skill can be unclear without a structure, validation steps, and packaging rules. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical workflow for building and preparing skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to create or update a reusable AI skill.
Defines the expected SKILL.md structure and required metadata.
Provides guidance for scripts, references, and assets folders.
Includes an initialization script that scaffolds a new skill directory.
Includes a packaging script that validates and zips a skill folder.
Lists practical best practices for testing and improving skills.
limitations: It does not publish skills to a marketplace or registry.
It does not verify third-party code inside generated skills.
It depends on local Python execution for scaffolding and packaging.
It provides general guidance, not platform-specific policy approval.

use_cases.title: Create a New Internal Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated team workflow into a reusable skill with clear triggers, instructions, and supporting files.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Skill for Review
use_cases.description: Check required metadata, folder structure, documentation quality, and packaging readiness before sharing a skill.

use_cases.title: Refactor Existing Instructions
use_cases.description: Move long reference material out of SKILL.md and organize scripts, references, and assets more clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new skill for [task]. Ask for the examples you need, then propose the SKILL.md outline and folder structure.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Draft Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my draft skill for trigger clarity, workflow completeness, and unnecessary duplication. Suggest specific edits before packaging.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Bundled Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these usage examples, decide what belongs in SKILL.md, scripts, references, and assets. Explain the structure and why each file is needed.

prompt_templates.title: Package and Validate a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this skill for marketplace readiness. Check metadata, resource organization, validation warnings, packaging risk, and missing user guidance.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for generating monthly finance reports from a standard spreadsheet.
output: A concise skill purpose and trigger description.
A proposed workflow for reading inputs, validating fields, and producing the report.
A folder plan with references for report rules and scripts for repeatable checks.

output_examples.input: Review my existing support-triage skill before I share it with my team.
output: A quality review covering trigger clarity, workflow gaps, and resource placement.
A list of changes to complete before packaging.
A short note about local scripts and files that users should inspect.

best_practices: Start from real user prompts so triggers match actual usage.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move long details into references.
Review generated scripts and packaged files before sharing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad skills that trigger for unrelated tasks.
Do not leave TODO placeholders in metadata or user-facing instructions.
Do not package local files without checking what the archive includes.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create reusable AI skills with clear instructions, metadata, optional scripts, references, and assets.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Python helper scripts for scaffolding a skill and packaging a skill directory into a zip file.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is suitable for publication with a medium warning because it writes local files and creates archives.

faq.question: Can it publish a skill automatically?
faq.answer: No. It prepares local skill files and archives, but it does not upload or publish them.

faq.question: What should users review before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should review generated scripts, remove unused examples, complete TODO text, and inspect files included in the archive.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
